zh.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron是Aaron FitzPayne的女兒，於1155年出生。她和Idnerth ap Cadwgan至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron是Aaron FitzPayne的女儿，于1155年出生。她和Idnerth ap Cadwgan至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Aaron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, West Indian, Guyanese, and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Aharon (which was Latinized as Aaron), borne by the first high priest of the Israelites, the brother of Moses (Exodus 4:14). Like Moses, it is probably of Egyptian origin, with a meaning no longer recoverable. In England and Wales, the name comes from the occasional adoption of Aaron as a Christian personal name. In south Wales, for example, where fixed surnames developed much later than in England, it was coined independently as a surname in the 17th–19th centuries, reflecting the enthusiasm for Old Testament personal names among Nonconformists.

variants.name: Aaronson
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name   Aaron  .

variants.name: Aronin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Aron, Hyman, Isaak, Khaim.  Russian Boris, Gennadiy, Grigoriy, Oleg.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Aron, a variant of Aaron . It is formed with the Slavic possessive suffix -in.

variants.name: Aronov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Mikhail, Igor, Vladimir, Yuriy, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Arkady, Iosif, Yefim, Anatoly, Auram.  Jewish Aron, Yakov, Uziel, Ari, Gavriel, Isaak, Khaim, Mikhel, Moshe, Naum, Rimma.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): patronymic from Aron, an East Slavic form of the personal name Aaron . It is formed with the Slavic possessive suffix -ov.

variants.name: Aarons
meanings: Jewish: patronymic from   Aaron  .

variants.name: Aharon
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): variant of   Aaron  .

variants.name: Arkin
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): patronymic from Arke, an eastern Yiddish pet form of the personal name Aaron . It is formed with the Slavic possessive suffix -in.

variants.name: Arone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic.
Italian: from the Biblical personal name Aron(n)e (see Aaron ).

variants.name: Agron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Aida, Alfredo, Ana, Andres, Angel, Elena, Erlinda, Francisco, Gonzalo, Isidoro, Jose.  Jewish Meyer, Samoil.
Galician and Spanish: habitational name from either of two places called Agrón, in A Coruña and Granada.

Jewish (American and Israeli): shortened form, with the Russian patronymic suffix (such as -sky, -ovsky, -ovich, or -ov) dropped, of some original eastern Ashkenazic surname based on the Jewish male personal name Ahron, a variant of Aaron . Spelling with g is due to Russian influence, since Russian has no h and alters it to g in borrowed words and names.

variants.name: Arnold
meanings: English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Hungarian, Czech, Slovak, Polish, Croatian, and Slovenian: from the ancient Germanic personal name Arnwald (Middle English Arnold, Old French Arnaut), composed of the elements arn ‘eagle’ + wald ‘rule, power’. This name was introduced to Britain by the Normans.
English: habitational name from either of two places called Arnold in Nottinghamshire and East Yorkshire, from Old English earn ‘eagle’ + halh ‘nook’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of the German personal name (see 1 above), at least in part on account of its resemblance to the Jewish name   Aaron  .

variants.name: Aron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Jakob, Mordche, Oded, Eliyahu, Girsh, Hersh, Shoshana, Tauba.
Jewish, English, and Scottish: variant of   Aaron  .
German: from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Aron (see Aaron ).

en.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron was born in 1155 as the daughter of Aaron FitzPayne. She had at least 1 son with Idnerth ap Cadwgan.

fr.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron est née en 1155, fille de Aaron FitzPayne. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Idnerth ap Cadwgan.

de.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron wurde 1155, als Tochter von Aaron FitzPayne geboren. Sie hatte mit Idnerth ap Cadwgan mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron è nata il nel 1155 come figlia di Aaron FitzPayne. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Idnerth ap Cadwgan.

ja.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaronは1155年にAaron FitzPayneの娘として生まれた。彼女はIdnerth ap Cadwganとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron(은)는 1155년 Aaron FitzPayne의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Idnerth ap Cadwgan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron nasceu em 1155 filha de Aaron FitzPayne. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Idnerth ap Cadwgan.

ru.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron родилась в 1155 г. Её отца звали Aaron FitzPayne. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Idnerth ap Cadwgan.

es.lifeSummary: Dyddgu verch Aaron nació en 1155 como hija de Aaron FitzPayne. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Idnerth ap Cadwgan.
